title: Stop the ICC Act
plain_summary: This bill would stop the United States from working with the International Criminal Court. It would also cut off money that America gives to the Palestinian Authority. The bill says that no federal government money can be used to help or support the International Criminal Court in any way. This means the US would completely end its cooperation and financial support for these organizations.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Stop the ICC Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Findings
sections.text: Congress has determined these facts. Since two thousand fourteen, Congress has passed laws every year that say economic support money cannot be given to the Palestinian Authority if the Palestinians start or actively help an International Criminal Court investigation. This rule applies when the investigation looks into Israeli citizens for crimes they might have committed against Palestinians. On May twenty-second, two thousand eighteen, the Palestinian Authority started an International Criminal Court investigation with permission from the court. They have actively supported this investigation, which looks into crimes that Israeli citizens might have committed against Palestinians. The International Criminal Court says that on May twenty-second, two thousand eighteen, Palestine asked the court to investigate past, current, and future crimes in all parts of Palestinian territory. This territory includes the West Bank, East Jerusalem, and the Gaza Strip, which are areas that Israel occupied in nineteen sixty-seven. In two thousand twenty-one, the International Criminal Court opened an investigation called the Situation in Palestine. On May twentieth, two thousand twenty-four, the chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court asked for arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and former Defense Minister Yoav Gallant. The prosecutor claims they committed crimes in Gaza starting at least on October eighth, two thousand twenty-three. On November twenty-first, two thousand twenty-four, the court rejected challenges from Israel about the arrest warrants and about whether the court has authority over this matter. On November twenty-first, two thousand twenty-four, the International Criminal Court issued arrest warrants for Prime Minister Netanyahu and former Defense Minister Gallant.

sections.title: Section 3  Prohibition Against Cooperation With the International Criminal Court and Support for the Palestinian Authority
sections.text: No matter what any other law says, no United States government official may cooperate with the International Criminal Court on any matter. The investigation mentioned earlier and the request for arrest warrants for Israeli officials gives good reason to stop using economic support money for the Palestinian Authority, as required by existing law. None of the money from the Economic Support Fund can be used to help the Palestinian Authority. No matter what any other law says, no federal government money can be given to the International Criminal Court or used to support any International Criminal Court activities. This includes submitting claims about crimes, starting investigations, conducting investigations, issuing warrants, transferring accused people, prosecuting cases, holding trials, handling appeals, or enforcing court decisions.
